reasoning: The custom `countably_compact` definition correctly formalizes "every countable open cover has a finite subcover". The theorem's RHS captures all four components of the informal statement: each C n is closed (`IsClosed (C n)`), nonempty (`C n ≠ ∅`), nested (`C (n+1) ⊆ C n`, matching $C_1 \supset C_2 \supset \cdots$), and the conclusion `∃ x, ∀ n, x ∈ C n` expresses nonempty intersection. The biconditional structure matches the informal "if and only if". Starting indexing at 0 rather than 1 is mathematically inconsequential. No extraneous hypotheses are added and no required ones are dropped.
